label: fraud
narrative: Atlas Logistics SL invoiced Helios Trading Ltd for EUR 1,250,000 in 'consulting services' on 2026-01-15. Helios re-invoiced Atlas for EUR 1,238,500 in 'logistics analytics' on 2026-01-19. Both companies share the same registered address (Calle Mayor 12, Madrid) and the sole director of Helios was an Atlas employee until 2025-11. No deliverables or work products exist on either side. Bank statements show the funds moved Atlas -> Helios -> Atlas with a 0.9% spread.
ledger_excerpt:
2026-01-15 DR Consulting expense 1,250,000 / CR AP-Helios 1,250,000
2026-01-19 DR AR-Helios 1,238,500 / CR Service revenue 1,238,500
2026-01-22 Bank-out 1,250,000 to Helios
2026-01-26 Bank-in 1,238,500 from Helios

label: clean
narrative: Standard freight invoice issued to Carrefour Espana for EUR 84,300 covering 17 routes Madrid-Barcelona between 2026-02-01 and 2026-02-28. Delivery notes signed, fuel receipts and toll records attached. Customer paid on 2026-03-15 within agreed 30-day terms. Margin in line with route average (8.4%).
ledger_excerpt:
2026-02-28 DR AR-Carrefour 84,300 / CR Freight revenue 84,300
2026-03-15 DR Bank 84,300 / CR AR-Carrefour 84,300

label: fraud
narrative: On 2026-12-30, NovaPharma SA shipped EUR 4.2M of product to 6 distributors with extended payment terms (180 days, vs the standard 30) and an undocumented verbal right of return. Q4 revenue was booked, hitting analyst targets. In January 2026, 78% of the shipped product was returned. CFO emails (subpoenaed) discuss 'parking inventory until the audit closes'.
ledger_excerpt:
2026-12-30 DR AR-Distributors 4,200,000 / CR Pharma revenue 4,200,000
2027-01-15 DR Sales returns 3,276,000 / CR AR-Distributors 3,276,000

label: suspicious
narrative: Senior partner submitted EUR 38,420 in T&E expenses in Q2 vs a 4-quarter average of EUR 9,200. Receipts include 14 client dinners with no client name, a 6-night stay at Hotel Arts Barcelona during a week with no recorded client meetings, and EUR 11,200 in 'miscellaneous office supplies' paid to a vendor incorporated 2026-04-01 with no website.
ledger_excerpt: 2026-04-01 ... 2026-06-30: T&E batches totalling 38,420 split across 47 transactions

label: clean
narrative: Q3 2026 impairment of EUR 1.8M against the value of obsolete manufacturing equipment for a discontinued product line. Recognised on 2026-09-30 following an independent valuation by Grant Thornton dated 2026-09-22. Disclosed in the Q3 management report and matches the impairment indicators in IAS 36 (decline in market value, technological obsolescence).
ledger_excerpt: 2026-09-30 DR Impairment loss 1,800,000 / CR Accumulated impairment 1,800,000

label: fraud
narrative: Payroll register added 14 new 'field security officers' between 2026-01 and 2026-06, all with identical net salary (EUR 1,840), all paid to bank accounts at the same provincial credit union, and 11 of 14 share the registered home address of the HR manager's brother-in-law. No corresponding social security contributions filed for these IDs at the public registry.
ledger_excerpt: 2026-01-31 to 2026-06-30 DR Wages 154,560 / CR Payroll bank 154,560 (across 6 monthly cycles)

label: clean
narrative: EUR/USD forward contract entered on 2026-04-10 with Banco Santander to hedge USD 850,000 of expected freight receivables from US customers (settlement 2026-07-10). Hedge documentation, effectiveness test and management approval all on file. Mark-to-market loss of EUR 7,400 booked to OCI as the hedge qualifies for cash flow hedge accounting under IFRS 9.
ledger_excerpt: 2026-06-30 DR OCI - hedging reserve 7,400 / CR Derivative liability 7,400

label: fraud
narrative: Three invoices totalling EUR 980,000 booked as revenue from 'Belmont Holdings LLC' (Delaware shell, no operating address, no web presence). No goods or services were shipped. Belmont Holdings was incorporated 2026-01-08 by an attorney who has incorporated 142 entities in the same week. Cash was never collected; the receivable was written off in Q4 as 'doubtful'.
ledger_excerpt:
2026-01-20 DR AR-Belmont 410,000 / CR Service revenue 410,000
2026-02-05 DR AR-Belmont 320,000 / CR Service revenue 320,000
2026-03-11 DR AR-Belmont 250,000 / CR Service revenue 250,000
2026-12-20 DR Bad debt expense 980,000 / CR AR-Belmont 980,000
expected_fraud_indicators: write-off pattern consistent with fictitious sales

label: suspicious
narrative: On 2026-12-31 at 23:47, a manual top-side journal entry of EUR 220,000 reclassified 'Provision for doubtful accounts' to 'Other operating income'. Posted by the CFO directly (not the usual accounting clerk), with the comment field empty and no supporting workpaper attached. The entry pushed reported EBITDA above the bank covenant threshold by EUR 35,000.
ledger_excerpt: 2026-12-31 23:47 DR Provision for doubtful accounts 220,000 / CR Other operating income 220,000

label: clean
narrative: Standard Q1 2026 payroll run for 312 employees totalling EUR 1,124,500 gross. Headcount and average salary in line with prior quarter (+0.4%). All employees match the social security registry, all payments to individual personal accounts, no duplicates detected by the automated control. Quarterly 111 model filed on time with the Spanish tax authority.
ledger_excerpt: 2026-03-31 DR Wages 1,124,500 / CR Payroll bank 824,300 / CR Withholdings payable 300,200
